title: Source Control (Perforce/Git)
description: Master version control structures: Perforce (P4) locks, Git LFS for binary files, merge conflicts, and branching workflows.
learningObjectives: Differentiate centralized systems (Perforce) from distributed systems (Git)
Manage large binary assets (textures, meshes) using Git LFS or Perforce depots
Resolve merge conflicts and handle file locks on binary assets

sections.title: Version Control for AAA Games
sections.content:
Real-time game projects contain huge binary assets (GB-sized textures, levels, models) that cannot be merged like text files. 

1. **Perforce (P4)**: Centralized. Standard in AAA. Supports exclusive file locking (`p4 checkout`), preventing two artists from editing the same binary asset simultaneously.
2. **Git**: Distributed. Lightweight for code. Requires Git LFS (Large File Storage) extension to redirect large binary pointers to external servers instead of bloating the code history size.

sections.title: Git LFS Config File (.gitattributes)
sections.content:
Configuring Git to route binary files through Large File Storage instead of direct repository commits:

```text
# Force binary mesh and texture extensions to use LFS pointers
*.uasset filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text
*.umap filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text
*.png filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text
*.fbx filter=lfs diff=lfs merge=lfs -text
```
